title: Lists (Arrays) 📋
description: Store an ordered line of items in a single variable.

slides.title: The Filing Cabinet 🗄️
slides.content:
# Grouping Items

Until now, a variable box could only hold ONE item at a time. `String name = 'Bob';`

What if you have a class of 30 students? You don't want to create 30 different variables: `name1`, `name2`, `name3`...

A **List** (known as an Array in other languages) lets you store hundreds of items in a single, ordered line!

slides.title: Creating a List 📝

quizData.question: What brackets are used to visually create a List?
quizData.explanation: Lists always use [ ] brackets to hold their separated items.

slides.title: Zero-Indexed Access 👆
slides.content:
## Counting starts at ZERO

To access a specific item in the List, we use its **index** (its position number).

But here is the catch: Computers start counting at `0`, NOT `1`!

`List names = ['Ash', 'Misty', 'Brock'];`
* Index `0` is 'Ash'
* Index `1` is 'Misty'
* Index `2` is 'Brock'

quizData.question:
Fill in the exact index number to print 'Misty' from the list above:
print( names[__] );

slides.title: See it in Action
slides.content: We can easily `.add()` and `.remove()` items from a List!
